## Corpora Considered

Machine-readable display table: [plot1_replication_source_display_table.csv](../data/derived/effect_inflation_dataset/plot1_replication_source_display_table.csv)

Full audit catalog: [plot1_replication_source_catalog.csv](../data/derived/effect_inflation_dataset/plot1_replication_source_catalog.csv); normalization worklist: [plot1_replication_source_normalization_worklist.csv](../data/derived/effect_inflation_dataset/plot1_replication_source_normalization_worklist.csv)

Plot 1 currently includes `57` source families in the rendered Figure 2 rule subset, has `3` integrated live source families that contribute zero rows after the rule gate, tracks `1` support-only integrated sources, and leaves `26` checked families out of the rendered plot.

This table is normalized to source families for reading. The full audit catalog keeps lower-level artifacts, aliases, lead handles, and project slices so the roll-up is reversible.

::: {.dataset-table .source-audit-table .combined-source-audit-table}

| Source considered | What it is/Why Possible Candidate | Why in/out | Kept<br>for Figure | No<br>D/Z | No<br>N | N<br>< 10 | Rep N <=<br>Orig N |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FReD [@tosh2024] | FORRT/FReD is a living replication database that aggregates large-scale replication projects, public replication lists, and individual submissions. Rows pair a replication study with the original study it targets, mostly psychology and adjacent social/behavioral claims. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 843 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 685 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: 843 structured local pair row(s) were present and 685 pass the Figure 2 D/N and size gates. FReD is not a random journal-paper denominator, so only D/N-convertible rows that clear the size gates are plotted. | 685 | 0 | 0 | 8 | 150 |
| SCORE project [@tyner2026] | DARPA SCORE/COS social and behavioral reproducibility corpus. The local join links original paper claims to version-of-record replication outcomes from the SCORE evaluation workflow. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 282 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 171 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 282 structured local pair row(s) were present and 171 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some rows are below the minimum-N floor; some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Built from `orig_outcomes.csv` joined to version-of-record rows in `repli_outcomes.csv`; reproduces the 282-row appendix-style join exactly. | 171 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 110 |
| LOOPR [@soto2019loopr] | Life Outcomes of Personality Replication project workbook. It codes original and replication effects for personality-outcome associations, with Fisher-z and bounded numeric fallbacks converted into D/N pair rows. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 118 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 104 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 118 structured local pair row(s) were present and 104 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Integrated from the public coding workbook using outcome-level Fisher-z columns first, then bounded numeric effect fallbacks. | 104 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 14 |
| Reproducibility Project: Psychology [@collaboration2015] | Open Science Collaboration psychology replication project: 100 psychology studies sampled from three journals and replicated by many teams. Local rows come from the canonical OSF data plus a small manual project-record fallback. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 100 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 67 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 100 structured local pair row(s) were present and 67 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some rows are below the minimum-N floor; some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Canonical raw CSV; build now reads the `T_r..O.`/`T_r..R.` and `T_N..O.`/`T_N..R.` columns directly. | 67 | 0 | 0 | 5 | 28 |
| Coppock 2019 generalizability corpus [@coppock2019generalizability] | Coppock's political-science generalizability project reanalyzes published survey experiments in new MTurk and GfK samples. The Dataverse archive includes standardized original and replication treatment-effect estimates plus stacked study data for model-specific sample sizes. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 50 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 35 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 50 structured local pair row(s) were present and 35 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 35 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 15 |
| Student Replication Projects [@boyce2023studentreplication] | Boyce/Mathur/Frank student replication corpus: classroom and training-course replications of published social/behavioral findings, curated as target-versus-replication effect pairs. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 114 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 35 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 114 structured local pair row(s) were present and 35 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some rows are below the minimum-N floor; some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Current integration is conservative: only rows with explicit `target_d_calc`, `rep_d_calc`, and both sample sizes are used. | 35 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 78 |
| Reproducibility Project: Cancer Biology [@errington2021] | Cancer Biology replication project: preclinical experimental claims selected from influential cancer-biology papers and replicated by independent laboratories. Local rows use the project effect-level analysis file. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 118 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 33 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 118 structured local pair row(s) were present and 33 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some rows are below the minimum-N floor; some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Still the canonical local effect-level replication file. | 33 | 0 | 0 | 56 | 29 |
| Clinical phase II to phase III pairs [@internalKerschbaumerPhaseIiIiiRheumatology] | Clinical phase II to phase III pairs is a harvest-promoted pair table source. The source contributes structured original-versus-replication D/N rows. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 32 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 32 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 32 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 32 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 32 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Communication privacy replications [@masur2025privacy] | Communication-privacy replication corpus covering SEM/specification-curve paths in media and communication research. Local rows are promoted from project source files into pair-level D/N rows. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 31 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 31 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 31 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 31 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 31 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Many Labs 2 [@klein2018] | Many Labs 2 multi-lab psychology replication project. The local join combines public replication summary rows with original-effect files to form original-versus-replication pairs. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 28 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 28 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: 28 structured local pair row(s) were present and 28 pass the Figure 2 rule. Source-specific note: Built from public replication summary plus original-effects files. | 28 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| EPRP [@cova2021estimating] | Experimental Philosophy Replicability Project: coordinated replications of experimental-philosophy findings. Local rows use the project complete-data file and recover effect sizes from reported statistics where needed. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 34 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 27 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 34 structured local pair row(s) were present and 27 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some rows are below the minimum-N floor; some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Direct project CSV; effect sizes are parsed directly or recovered from numeric analysis strings. | 27 | 0 | 0 | 2 | 5 |
| Decision-Market Replication Project [@holzmeister2025decisionmarket] | Decision-market replication project in experimental economics and behavioral science. The public table contains original and replication D/N fields plus market forecasts. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 26 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 26 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: 26 structured local pair row(s) were present and 26 pass the Figure 2 rule. Source-specific note: Uses the harmonized public decision-market replication table with original and replication d and N columns. | 26 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Social Sciences Replication Project [@camerer2018] | Social Sciences Replication Project: coordinated replications of social-science studies from high-profile journals. Local rows use ReplicationSuccess plus a few manual/support rows. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 26 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 23 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 26 structured local pair row(s) were present and 23 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Full 21-row SSRP object with original and replication r, Fisher-z, p-values, and Ns. | 23 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 3 |
| Brazilian Reproducibility Initiative [@amaral2019brazilian] | Brazilian Reproducibility Initiative: coordinated replications of experimental psychology findings by Brazilian labs. Local rows use project primary-analysis outputs and exclude sensitivity variants. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 45 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 21 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 45 structured local pair row(s) were present and 21 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some rows are below the minimum-N floor; some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Built from the public BRI repo plus locally generated primary-analysis output; excludes `ALT*` sensitivity variants and preserves the project-native experiment effect metric (`ROM`/`MD`). | 21 | 0 | 0 | 20 | 4 |
| Management Science Replication Project [@davis2023managementscience] | Management Science Replication Project: replications of operations and management-science experiments. Local rows are parsed from project report tables into original-versus-replication effect pairs. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 26 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 20 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 26 structured local pair row(s) were present and 20 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some rows are below the minimum-N floor; some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 20 | 0 | 0 | 4 | 2 |
| Many Labs 5 [@ebersole2020manylabs5] | Many Labs 5 multi-lab psychology replication project. Local rows use the public figure data and single-effect files for version-specific sample sizes. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 20 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 20 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: 20 structured local pair row(s) were present and 20 pass the Figure 2 rule. Source-specific note: Build uses `ML5FigureData.csv` for ES and the single-effects file for version-specific Ns; adds ML5 RP:P and ML5 Revised arrows. | 20 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Experimental Economics Replication Project [@camerer2016] | Experimental Economics Replication Project, a coordinated replication of laboratory economics findings. Local rows use the Experimental Economics subset of the ReplicationSuccess RProjects object plus manual supplement rows. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 25 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 18 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 25 structured local pair row(s) were present and 18 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some rows are below the minimum-N floor; some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Uses the Experimental Economics subset of the harmonized 143-row RProjects object. | 18 | 0 | 0 | 2 | 5 |
| 251 Rescue Projects [@boyce2024rescue] | Rescue-project corpus of classic psychology findings and replication attempts. Local rows come from parsed rescue files after removing exact overlap with the Boyce student-replication source. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 30 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 17 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 30 structured local pair row(s) were present and 17 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Built from parsed and combined rescue files, then de-duplicated against exact Boyce overlap keys before integration. | 17 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 13 |
| Sports science replications [@murphy2025sportsReplication] | Sports-science replication corpus. Public supplemental reported-effect tables are joined to sample-size workbooks and kept where both original and replication sides are numeric. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 19 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 16 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 19 structured local pair row(s) were present and 16 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some rows are below the minimum-N floor; some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Build joins the public reported-effect supplemental table to the public sample-size workbook and retains only rows with numeric original and replication effect values; partial eta squared is converted onto the common D-like axis and standardized-mean-difference variants are preserved on that scale. | 16 | 0 | 0 | 2 | 1 |
| Awesome replicability-data repository [@jinAwesomeReplicabilityData] | Jin/Ying awesome-replicability-data is a GitHub collection of cleaned paired original-and-replication raw datasets for individual direct-replication examples. Local rows use source-specific parsers for the paired CSVs that expose a clear condition, outcome, original sample, and replication sample. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 12 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 11 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 12 structured local pair row(s) were present and 11 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 11 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 1 |
| Independent Replication Initiative [@schweinsberg2016pipeline] | Independent Replication Initiative source family, a collection of independently run psychology/social-science direct replications. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 10 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 10 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: 10 structured local pair row(s) were present and 10 pass the Figure 2 rule. Source-specific note: Nine rows are converted from supplement-reported key-test statistics; Intuitive Economics uses the packet CSV to recover the replication-side correlation directly. | 10 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Many Labs 1 [@klein2014manylabs1] | Many Labs 1 multi-lab psychology replication project, harmonized by Altmejd. It contains many-site replications of classic psychology effects with aggregate original and replication effect information. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 10 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 10 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: 10 structured local pair row(s) were present and 10 pass the Figure 2 rule. Source-specific note: Used because the ML1 summary workbook does not expose a clean original-N row for every effect. | 10 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Many Labs 3 [@ebersole2016manylabs3] | Many Labs 3 multi-lab psychology replication project, using Altmejd's harmonized project tables where effect sizes and sample sizes are pair-buildable. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 10 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 10 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: 10 structured local pair row(s) were present and 10 pass the Figure 2 rule. Source-specific note: Used because the ML3 manuscript tables expose ES cleanly but not a ready original-N row structure. | 10 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Public-administration blame experiment replication [@walker2024publicAdminBlame] | Public-administration replication of the James et al. blame-attribution experiment. Local rows are paper-table reconstructions from the original James et al. 2016 regression table and Walker et al. 2024 England, Hong Kong, and South Korea replication tables. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 9 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 9 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 9 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 9 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: The OSF registration did not expose raw respondent data, but the Wiley article and supporting tables report matched treatment-contrast t statistics and balanced arm sizes. Those tables now supply 9 promoted D/N reconstruction rows. | 9 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Clinical highly cited replications [@ioannidis2005contradicted] | Clinical-medicine replication workbook pairing highly cited early clinical findings with later larger or contradictory evidence; local rows keep medical effects where OR, RR, or HR plus sample sizes can be converted. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 7 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 7 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: 7 structured local pair row(s) were present and 7 pass the Figure 2 rule. Source-specific note: Limited to rows with convertible `OR`, `RR`, or `HR` outcomes plus both original and replication sample sizes. | 7 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| EQIPD Arroyo-Araujo 2022 [@arroyoaraujo2022eqipd] | EQIPD preclinical multi-lab project on replicability and generalizability of open-field findings under protocol harmonization. Local rows are stagewise lab contrasts converted to D/N where possible. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 7 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 7 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 7 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 7 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 7 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Appendix D supplemental [@douglass2026appendixD] | Hand-built appendix source used as a coverage floor for undercovered replication families. It is a support/fallback source rather than an independent corpus. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: the source catalog marks 555 usable pair row(s) out of 555 cataloged row(s). | Confirmed usable fallback: 6 row(s) from 555 cataloged usable row(s) pass the Figure 2 rule through the catalog/backfill route rather than a separate live source table. Source-specific note: Used as a floor source for undercovered project families in the Figure 2 rule and plotted subsets. | 6 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| COVID online experiment replications [@peyton2021covidonline] | COVID-era online experiment replication source. Local rows use public summary files that pair original claims with online replication results. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 26 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 6 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 26 structured local pair row(s) were present and 6 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 6 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 22 |
| Contextual bias in verbal credibility assessment [@oppen2026contextual] | Direct replication source for contextual bias in verbal credibility assessment, a forensic/psychology credibility-judgment effect. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 6 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 6 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 6 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 6 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 6 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Linguistic frame effect on blame and liability [@tonkovic2022linguisticOsf] | Preregistered direct replication of the linguistic frame effect on perceived blame and financial liability in legal/psychology judgment. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 6 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 6 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 6 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 6 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 6 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Eyewitness memory distortion in ten countries [@ito2018eyewitnessTenCountries] | Cross-national eyewitness-memory replication/generalizability source on memory distortion across ten countries. The checked a35rx OSF node still has empty public storage, but the accepted-manuscript forest plot reports original New Zealand and country-level Hedges-g/N values, allowing a five-row paper-table rescue. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 5 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 5 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 5 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 5 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: The OSF payload remains unresolved, but the accepted manuscript reports Hedges-g values and analyzed Ns. Five country-level paper-fallback rows now pass the current gates; the nested aggregate row is intentionally not added. | 5 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Ying 2023 pilot-full-scale trials [@ying2023pilot] | Pilot-versus-full-scale trial source reconstructed from Ying's dissertation materials. One pilot/full-scale D/N pair is live; a local PubMed/open-access probe tightened five more candidate notes without finding enough public arm/variance information for promotion. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 5 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 5 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed partly usable: manual reconstruction recovered 5 pilot/full-scale D/N pair row(s), and 5 pass the figure gates; the remaining source material is roster-only, native-metric, or missing compatible variance. | 5 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| PSA 006 trolley Greene [@bago2022trolley] | Psychological Science Accelerator trolley-problem replication project, with country-level rows for the Greene trolley dilemma effect. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 90 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 4 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 90 structured local pair row(s) were present and 4 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some rows are below the minimum-N floor; some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 4 | 0 | 0 | 4 | 82 |
| RRR Colling 2020 [@rrrColling2020Osf] | Registered Replication Report for the Att-SNARC effect from Fischer et al. The local GitHub archive contains Fischer original estimates, processed replication meta-analysis outputs, and raw participant files; promoted rows use a within-subject dz-compatible table reconstruction. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 4 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 4 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 4 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 4 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: The local GitHub archive includes Fischer original dz values and the Colling et al. selected Model 1 fixed-effect z statistics. Four Att-SNARC ISI rows are now promoted using abs(z)/sqrt(N) as a within-subject dz-compatible replication proxy. | 4 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Eerland 2016 Hart intention-attribution replications [@eerland2016hart] | Eerland/Hart direct-replication source in social psychology, covering intention-attribution, intentionality, and imagery experiments with public per-study CSVs. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 3 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 3 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 3 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 3 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 3 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| RRR Alogna 2014 [@alogna2014schooler] | RRR Alogna 2014 is a registered-replication source in psychology, normally a multi-lab direct replication of one target effect; local rows use public site, aggregate, or manual source files where D/N is recoverable. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 3 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 3 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 3 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 3 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 3 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| RRR Elliott 2021 [@elliott2021flavell] | RRR Elliott 2021 is a registered-replication source in psychology, normally a multi-lab direct replication of one target effect; local rows use public site, aggregate, or manual source files where D/N is recoverable. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 3 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 3 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 3 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 3 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 3 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| RRR McCarthy 2018 [@mccarthy2018srull] | RRR McCarthy 2018 is a registered-replication source in psychology, normally a multi-lab direct replication of one target effect; local rows use public site, aggregate, or manual source files where D/N is recoverable. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 3 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 3 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 3 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 3 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 3 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| RRR Hagger 2016 [@hagger2016egodepletion] | RRR Hagger 2016 is a registered-replication source in psychology, normally a multi-lab direct replication of one target effect; local rows use public site, aggregate, or manual source files where D/N is recoverable. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 2 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 2 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 2 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 2 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 2 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Wood scaffolding direct replication [@smit2025scaffolding] | Direct replication source for a behavioral wood-scaffolding effect; local rows use participant-level source material where original and replication D/N can be paired. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 2 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 2 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 2 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 2 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 2 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Doyen et al. 2012 article (manual) [@doyen2012priming] | Manual extraction from Doyen et al.'s social-priming replication of elderly-prime walking-speed effects, used as a paper-grounded direct replication pair. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 1 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 1 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Galak et al. 2012 psi meta-analysis (manual) [@galak2012psi] | Manual extraction from Galak et al.'s replication/meta-analysis of Bem-style psi effects, a parapsychology limit-case direct-replication source. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 1 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 1 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Greed by SES replications [@balakrishnan2017greed] | Social-psychology direct-replication source testing the reported relationship between socioeconomic status and greed-related behavior. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 1 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 1 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Many Labs 4 [@klein2022manylabs4] | Many Labs 4 multi-lab psychology replication project. Local rows are recovered from public site/meta-analysis outputs where both original and replication sides can be paired. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 1 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 1 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Marek 2022 BWAS [@marek2022] | Large-sample brain-wide association study source used as a high-N comparison for brain-behavior association claims. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 1 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 1 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| O'Donnell 2018 site-level meta csvs [@odonnell2018dijksterhuis] | Registered-replication-style psychology source with site-level meta-analytic rows for the O'Donnell/Dijksterhuis target effect. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 1 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 1 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| PSA 002 object orientation [@chen2025objectOrientation] | PSA 002 object orientation is a Psychological Science Accelerator replication source: a multi-site psychology project with country/lab-level replication data for a named target effect. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 2 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 2 structured local pair row(s) were present and 1 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 1 |
| PSA 004 JTB Turri [@hall2024psa004Turri] | Psychological Science Accelerator replication source for the Turri justified-true-belief vignette effect. The checked erm92 project exposes a codebook while its data and analysis children are empty; n5b3w provides original/pretest support, but the published article tables allow one strict Darrel/Squirrel OR-based row rescue. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 1 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 1 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: The final Stage 2 OSF payload is still unresolved for region-level extraction, but the published article reports original OR/N and Darrel-vignette replication counts. One strict Darrel/Squirrel row is now promoted from article tables; the expected region rows still need OSF/analysis-folder extraction. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Protzko 2023 NHB [@protzko2024highrep] | High-replicability psychology replication source from Protzko and colleagues, with local rows recovered from project writeups. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 3 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 3 structured local pair row(s) were present and 1 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 2 |
| RRR Bouwmeester 2017 [@bouwmeester2017rand] | RRR Bouwmeester 2017 is a registered-replication source in psychology, normally a multi-lab direct replication of one target effect; local rows use public site, aggregate, or manual source files where D/N is recoverable. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 22 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable with exclusions: 22 structured local pair row(s) were present and 1 pass the Figure 2 rule; rows outside the plot fail because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 21 |

Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Ranehill 2015 power posing (manual) [@ranehill2015powerposing] | Manual extraction from the Ranehill et al. replication of power-posing effects on hormones and risk taking. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 1 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 1 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Stoevenbelt 2025 combined lab csv [@stoevenbelt2024johns] | Participant-level multi-lab replication source for stereotype-threat effects; local rows use the combined lab CSV and registered exclusions. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: 1 structured local pair row(s) were present and 1 pass the Figure 2 rule. Source-specific note: Public combined participant CSV is filtered with the registered exclusion logic, converted into women-only stereotype-threat versus low-threat control Cohen's d rows per lab, and anchored to the original Johns, Schmader, & Martens (2005) effect reported in the public preprint. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Transparent Psi Project [@kekecs2023tpp] | Transparent Psi Project is a high-powered multi-lab replication of Bem-style precognition/psi effects. Local rows use the combined raw project data to build the pooled erotic-trial hit-rate comparison against the original Bem study. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 1 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 1 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Vaidis 2024 prepared data subset [@vaidis2024dissonance] | Participant-level multi-lab cognitive-dissonance replication source, converted into per-lab induced-compliance contrasts. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: 1 structured local pair row(s) were present and 1 pass the Figure 2 rule. Source-specific note: Public prepared-data CSV is filtered with the project analysis-script exclusions and converted into one HC-CE vs LC-CE Cohen's d row per lab, anchored to the original Croyle & Cooper (1983) effect. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Wagenmakers 2016 site csv [@wagenmakers2016strack] | Registered Replication Report source for the Strack facial-feedback hypothesis, using public site-level rows. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because it explicitly links original findings to replication or follow-up estimates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 1 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed usable: the public materials were reduced to 1 auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pair row(s), with 1 passing the figure gates. Source-specific note: Public harvested source was staged and promoted into live D/N pair rows after source-specific parsing. | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Deceptive intentions verbal credibility replication [@kleinberg2018deceptive] | Forensic psychology direct-replication source on deceptive intentions and verbal credibility assessment. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because structured local pair rows exist, even though none pass this plot's current gates. Pair-count basis: 1 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 0 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed present but not usable for this figure: 1 structured D/N pair row(s) exist, but zero pass the Figure 2 rule because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 1 |
| Online image credibility [@haim2025imagecredibility] | Media and communication direct-replication source testing online image-credibility judgments. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because structured local pair rows exist, even though none pass this plot's current gates. Pair-count basis: 11 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 0 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed present but not usable for this figure: 11 structured D/N pair row(s) exist, but zero pass the Figure 2 rule because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 11 |
| Retrieval extinction fear conditioning in rats [@luyten2017retrievalExtinction] | Preclinical animal replication source for retrieval-extinction fear-conditioning effects in rats. Candidate rationale: treated as a likely pair source because structured local pair rows exist, even though none pass this plot's current gates. Pair-count basis: 3 structured local original/replication row(s) are in the live build; 0 pass the current Figure 2 rule. | Confirmed present but not usable for this figure: 3 structured D/N pair row(s) exist, but zero pass the Figure 2 rule because some follow-up samples are not larger than the original. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 3 |
| Manual paper-grounded pairs [@douglass2026manualPairs] | Manual recovery layer grounded in supplements, DOI targets, project records, and downloaded papers where automated tables missed appendix-aligned pairs. Candidate rationale: retained as a candidate support source because it can validate, alias, or backfill already represented pair families. Pair-count basis: the source catalog marks 26 usable pair row(s) out of 26 cataloged row(s). | Confirmed support-only rather than independent pair evidence: the material is useful as fallback, alias, or cross-check data, but overlaps with or supplements rows represented elsewhere. Local material: Manual paper-grounded pairs. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Angrist learning-curve replication corpus [@angristLearningCurve2023Openicpsr] | Angrist and colleagues' education replication corpus follows targeted-instruction implementation across country trials. The OpenICPSR package is now local with three Stata monitoring datasets, code, figures, and one output table. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 1 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. Source-specific note: The downloaded OpenICPSR package is a real machine-readable payload, but it reproduces implementation-monitoring outputs rather than original-versus-replication treatment-effect D/N rows. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Border 2019 candidate gene replication [@border2019] | Border et al. 2019 re-tests historical candidate-gene claims for major depression using large-scale GWAS-style genetic data. The local PDF is useful for native genetic summaries, but it is not a direct small-original larger-replication D/N table. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 150 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. The staged candidate count was 150 possible row(s). Source-specific note: The primary PDF is local and the source is real, but the AJP supplemental endpoint returned 403 here. The visible results are native genetic association summaries, not direct original-versus-replication D/N rows. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| EEF regrants systematic replication candidates [@eefProjectsIndex] | EEF regrants systematic replication candidates is an education or learning-intervention replication source, cataloged as a potential pair family rather than a broad paper sample. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 1 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed not pair-buildable for this plot: the checked files are not an original-versus-replication D/N pair table. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| EGAP Metaketa I [@metaketaIRepo] | EGAP Metaketa I is an EGAP Metaketa political-science field-experiment source; it is cataloged for replication/generalizability evidence but is not currently on the shared D/N pair axis. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 24 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. The staged candidate count was 24 possible row(s). | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| EGAP Metaketa III [@metaketaIIIProject] | EGAP Metaketa III is a coordinated natural-resource-governance field-experiment project. The local harvest now includes the parent replication package and Uganda, Brazil, and Costa Rica component payloads with R/Stata outputs. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 30 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. The staged candidate count was 30 possible row(s). Source-specific note: The parent replication package and several component payloads are now local, including site-estimate and R/Stata files. They are useful native field-experiment evidence, but not an original-versus-replication D/N table for this figure. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| EGAP Metaketa IV [@metaketaIVProject] | EGAP Metaketa IV is the community-policing Metaketa. The local harvest now includes integrated OSF xqd3v RDS result outputs, article tables, and selected processed country files. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 36 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. The staged candidate count was 36 possible row(s). Source-specific note: Integrated OSF xqd3v RDS outputs and article tables are now local. They are native community-policing field-experiment estimates and therefore belong in a native-metric or field-experiment lane, not the current original-versus-replication D/N plot. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| ERN Pe RRR OpenNeuro [@ernPeRrrOpenNeuro] | Registered Replication Report material for ERN/Pe psychometrics. OpenNeuro provides raw EEG and OSF 8cbua provides summary and analysis artifacts, but these are native ERP materials rather than a compact pair table. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 1 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. Source-specific note: Raw EEG and OSF summary artifacts are local, but they are replication-side ERP materials on native metrics. The original-side D/N anchor is still missing from the machine-readable payload. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| I4R public workbook [@i4rPublicWorkbook] | Institute for Replication public meta-database workbook covering computational reproducibility, coding errors, and robustness checks for economics and political-science papers. It is large and useful context, but its estimate-level sheet is coefficient/SE robustness material rather than new-sample original-versus-replication D/N pairs. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 6,719 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed no usable pair rows in the checked catalog: 6,719 catalog row(s) were present, but none were marked as auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pairs for this figure. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| IBL reproducible electrophysiology [@iblReproEphysAws] | IBL reproducible electrophysiology is a neuroscience replication/generalizability source, cataloged for potential pair construction where a common effect-size and N policy is available. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 1 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| IES systematic replication awards [@iesSystematicReplicationAwards] | IES systematic replication awards is an education or learning-intervention replication source, cataloged as a potential pair family rather than a broad paper sample. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 1 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed not pair-buildable for this plot: the checked files are not an original-versus-replication D/N pair table. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Jaljuli Kafkafi multi lab rodent replication [@jaljuliKafkafi2023MousePhenome] | Preclinical multi-lab rodent replication source from the Jaljuli/Kafkafi mouse-phenome materials. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 152 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source looks relevant, but the effect-size conversion policy is not settled enough to put it on the shared D/Z scale. The staged candidate count was 152 possible row(s). | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Kerschbaumer 2020 rheumatology phase II/III [@internalKerschbaumerPhaseIiIiiRheumatology] | Kerschbaumer 2020 rheumatology phase II/III is a cataloged replication or generalizability source. The local evidence has been staged but still needs parser, metric, or conversion work before promotion. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 205 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed no usable pair rows in the checked catalog: 205 catalog row(s) were present, but none were marked as auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pairs for this figure. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Li 2024 PD-1/PD-L1 phase II/III oncology [@internalLiPd1Pdl1PhaseIiIiiOncology] | Li 2024 PD-1/PD-L1 phase II/III oncology is a cataloged replication or generalizability source. The local evidence has been staged but still needs parser, metric, or conversion work before promotion. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 51 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed no usable pair rows in the checked catalog: 51 catalog row(s) were present, but none were marked as auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pairs for this figure. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| ManyBabies 3 [@manyBabies3Osf] | ManyBabies 3 is a multi-lab developmental-psychology replication of Marcus-style infant rule learning. The local OSF pull contains registered-report PDFs, appendices, and a lab manual; the public mb3-rules GitHub archive adds stimuli, pilot materials, syllable ratings, and analysis notebooks. A 2026 manuscript/preprint trail appears to exist, but no public machine-readable final result repository is confirmed locally. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 15 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable: downloaded materials exist, but they do not contain a machine-readable original-versus-replication pair-results table for this plot. The staged candidate count was 15 possible row(s). Source-specific note: The public materials are useful for protocol and pilot auditing, but the final infant site-level result data are not public in the checked locations. The reported mb3-analysis repository returned 404; a final manuscript/preprint trail improves the lead but still does not provide a confirmed row-ready repository. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| ManyClasses 2 [@manyClasses2Osf] | ManyClasses 2 is a multi-classroom education experiment that repeated the same classroom intervention across course sections. The local files contain Terracotta-style participant outcomes and Bayesian analysis scripts, but not an explicit original-versus-replication D/N pair table. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 30 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. The staged candidate count was 30 possible row(s). | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Marcus Four Lab Replication Hcmv7 [@internalMarcusFourLabReplicationLead] | Marcus Four Lab Replication Hcmv7 is a cataloged replication or generalizability source. The local evidence is currently raw source material, not a structured D/N pair table. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: no concrete pair-row count was verified before checking the source. | Could not confirm usable pairs yet: downloaded materials exist, but they have not been staged into structured source rows, so row-level D/Z and N fit is still unverified. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Media priming direct replication [@mediaPriming2017Dataverse] | Political-communication/media-psychology preregistered replication data for one media-priming target effect. Dataverse identifies the intended Excel file, but the file is restricted; the public Cambridge appendix is local as a paper fallback and appears to recover replication N, not the target effect estimate. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 1 possible pair row(s). | Could not confirm usable pairs: access or download resolution is still blocked for the actual data payload. The staged candidate count was 1 possible row(s). Source-specific note: The Dataverse metadata identifies media_priming.xls, but the file is restricted and the direct API returns a blocked payload. The public Cambridge DOCX appendix is local as a fallback and appears to recover N_rep=104, but not the effect estimate needed for a D/N row. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Mullinix Leeper Druckman Freese 2015 [@mullinix2015Dataverse] | Mullinix, Leeper, Druckman, and Freese's survey-experiment generalizability project compares treatment effects across convenience, student, staff, MTurk, and TESS/GfK samples. The public Dataverse bundle contains combined data, scripts, and result logs, but the effects remain native survey-scale contrasts. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 20 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. The staged candidate count was 20 possible row(s). | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Musicianship EEG multi site replication [@musicianshipEegDataverse] | Large-scale multi-site neuroscience study of whether musical training is associated with early auditory neural encoding. The local GitHub zip contains MATLAB analysis code and processed EEG summaries rather than a smaller-original/larger-replication pair table. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 5 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. The staged candidate count was 5 possible row(s). | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| News discernment replication [@newsDiscernmentReplicationOsf] | OSF materials for a France/Germany news-discernment or misinformation intervention replication, with country-level clean survey workbooks and analysis scripts. The downloaded files are intervention datasets rather than a ready smaller-original/larger-replication D/N pair table. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 1 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed not pair-buildable for this plot: the checked files are not an original-versus-replication D/N pair table. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Nieuwland 2018 DeLong replication [@nieuwland2018delong] | Psycholinguistics multi-lab replication source for the DeLong sentence-comprehension effect. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 9 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source looks relevant, but the effect-size conversion policy is not settled enough to put it on the shared D/Z scale. The staged candidate count was 9 possible row(s). | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| PooledMarketR PM_data.Rdata [@pooledMarketRPackageData] | PooledMarketR package data with outcome, prediction-market, and survey metadata across EERP, Many Labs 2, RPP, and SSRP-style replication projects. It supports market/forecast checks and overlap audits, but it is not a richer original-versus-replication effect-size pair table. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 111 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed no usable pair rows in the checked catalog: 111 catalog row(s) were present, but none were marked as auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pairs for this figure. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Self control neuroimaging replication [@selfControlFmriOsf] | Self-control neuroimaging replication source for the Hare et al. dietary self-control fMRI paradigm. OSF materials, OpenNeuro raw-data leads, and an open paper mirror are local, but no final pair-results table is exposed. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 4 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable: downloaded materials exist, but they do not contain a machine-readable original-versus-replication pair-results table for this plot. The staged candidate count was 4 possible row(s). Source-specific note: OSF materials, raw fMRI leads, and an open paper mirror are local, but no final machine-readable table pairs the Hare original result with the replication on the shared D/N axis. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Strategy situation fit replication [@strategyFitOsf] | Social/personality psychology replication source for strategy-situation fit effects. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 3 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source looks relevant, but the effect-size conversion policy is not settled enough to put it on the shared D/Z scale. The staged candidate count was 3 possible row(s). | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Wils 2023 IBD phase II/III [@internalWilsIbdPhaseIiIii] | Wils 2023 IBD phase II/III is a cataloged replication or generalizability source. The local evidence has been staged but still needs parser, metric, or conversion work before promotion. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: 37 catalog row(s) were checked, but none are marked usable pair rows. | Confirmed no usable pair rows in the checked catalog: 37 catalog row(s) were present, but none were marked as auditable original-versus-replication D/Z and N pairs for this figure. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Wood and Porter elusive backfire effect [@woodPorter2019Dataverse] | Political-communication replication source for Wood and Porter's elusive backfire-effect experiments. The Dataverse bundle is local with survey and correction files plus R scripts; the suggested OSF pes2y mirror was checked and is a different fake-news memory project. Candidate rationale: tracked because the source was described in the audit trail as a replication, direct-replication, multi-lab, pilot/full-scale, or repeated-study source that might contain pairable evidence. Pair-count basis: the staged lead manifest expected 52 possible pair row(s). | Confirmed present but not usable on the current axis: the source is real, but its native metric does not sit cleanly on the shared D/Z scale and is better suited to a native-metric lane. The staged candidate count was 52 possible row(s). Source-specific note: The Dataverse bundle is local and rich, but the direct original-target pairing and shared-D conversion policy still need a native-metric or hand-extraction pass. The suggested OSF pes2y mirror was not this project. | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |

:::

## Specific Observations Included

Machine-readable current pair-level file: [FIGURE1_REPLICATION_PAIRS.tsv](../FIGURE1_REPLICATION_PAIRS.tsv)

The current strict Plot 1 / Figure 1A layer has `1,736` rows. It records the source, smaller-`N` and larger-`N` paper labels/DOIs where available, endpoint, each side's `D` and `N`, and `D_larger_N_minus_smaller_N`.

Preview of the first specific pair rows in source order:

::: {.dataset-table .specific-observation-table .replication-observation-table}

| Source | Smaller-N paper | Larger-N paper | Outcome | Smaller-N D/N | Larger-N D/N | D diff |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Birch & Bloom 2007 | Birch & Bloom 2007 (additional) | t(149.58)=-1.20 | 0.47 / 103 | 0.19 / 152 | -0.27 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Birch & Bloom 2007 | Birch & Bloom 2007 (rescue) | t(237.01)=-1.4687 | 0.47 / 103 | 0.19 / 247 | -0.28 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Child et al. 2018 | Child et al. 2018 (rep1) | MSD2:m1=1860(1510),m2=1960(1490) | 0.11 / 35 | 0.07 / 40 | -0.05 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Child et al. 2018 | Child et al. 2018 (rescue) | MSD2:m1=1930(1400),m2=1930(1400) | 0.11 / 35 | 0.00 / 98 | -0.11 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Chou et al. 2016 | Chou et al. 2016 (rescue) | MSD2:m1=11.52066(18.72659),m2=10.39695(16.53978) | 0.42 / 100 | 0.06 / 252 | -0.35 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Craig & Richeson 2014 | Craig & Richeson 2014 (rescue) | MSD2:m1=-.4307766(3.529429),m2=.4463469(3.848955) | 0.50 / 121 | 0.24 / 127 | -0.26 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Haimovitz & Dweck 2016 | Haimovitz & Dweck 2016 (rescue) | t(129.45)=-0.31382 | 0.56 / 132 | 0.05 / 141 | -0.51 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Hopkins et al. 2016 | Hopkins et al. 2016 (rescue) | MSD2:m1=1.177984(.6890454),m2=1.355208(.7170742) | 0.35 / 147 | 0.25 / 161 | -0.10 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Jara-Ettinger et al. 2022 | Jara-Ettinger et al. 2022 (rep1) | rawprop:91/147,chance=1/3 | 9.12 / 144 | 7.10 / 147 | -2.02 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Jara-Ettinger et al. 2022 | Jara-Ettinger et al. 2022 (rescue) | rawprop:191/426,chance=1/3 | 9.12 / 144 | 4.77 / 426 | -4.36 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Ngo et al. 2019 | Ngo et al. 2019 (additional) | MSE1:m1=.07(.01),comp=0 | 1.28 / 31 | 1.26 / 32 | -0.02 |
| 251 Rescue parsed data [@boyce2024rescue] | Ngo et al. 2019 | Ngo et al. 2019 (rescue) | MSE1:m1=.05(.06),comp=0 | 1.28 / 31 | 0.10 / 77 | -1.18 |

:::

### What Is Still Missing

- The remaining Plot 1 work is now substantive: access blocks, native-metric decisions, or missing machine-readable pair payloads.
- A few integrated families still retain artifact-level detail in the full audit catalog, but the reader-facing table is normalized to source families.
- Some support-only and cross-check sources still need a sharper distinction between `does not contribute rows` and `duplicate validation source`.
